feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain. Rainfall amounts may approach warning criteria, mainly about the ranges. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 80 to 110 mm of rain to accumulate about the ranges and coastal hills, and lesser amounts elsewhere. This is in addition to the rain that has already fallen. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h until tonight. Note, warning amounts are unlikely near the coast north of Cape Kidnappers. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal. Changes: Watch upgraded to an Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 70 to 90 mm of rain to accumulate about the ranges and coastal hills, and lesser amounts elsewhere. This is in addition to the rain that has already fallen. Peak rates of 5 to 15 mm/h. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: The chance of upgrade for this Orange Warning has been decreased to low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 50 to 70 mm of rain to accumulate about the ranges and coastal hills, and lesser amounts elsewhere. This is in addition to the rain that has already fallen. Peak rates of 5 to 15 mm/h. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal. Changes: Downgraded from a Red Warning to an Orange Warning, and period of heavy rain shortened.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Low. Changes: The chance of upgrade for this Watch has been decreased to low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: South to southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Low. Changes: The chance of upgrade for this Watch has been decreased to low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: From NOW until 9pm tonight snow possible above 800 metres with a risk of around 1 cm settling about the summit. A chance that snow flurries could continue overnight into Wednesday morning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: From NOW until mid Wednesday morning, snow showers above about 800 metres. A further 2 to 4 cm may accumulate about the summit.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain, falling as snow down to 1000 metres. Expect a small amount (1 to 2 cm) to accumulate on the road about the summit at times.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
